ships_in: v0.11 ships these targets PROVISIONAL by design — the release is not gated on the capacity baseline. The measured capacity/scaling numbers land in a follow-up MINOR (status flips to 'declared' then). v0.11 correctness floors are enforced now.
status_note: PROVISIONAL until a baseline run on the DECLARED hardware class fills the capacity/scaling numbers and an owner flips status to 'declared' (MCPRE-110). This is deliberately NOT a v0.11 blocker: v0.11 publishes the gate mechanism + enforced correctness floors + null (preliminary) capacity targets; the representative measured numbers arrive in a later minor. ADR-051 §7 is explicit that SLO numbers are measured, not asserted ('capacity claims without a pinned benchmark envelope are marketing, not engineering'), so the capacity/scaling targets below are null on purpose. The gate ENFORCES every non-null target and the correctness floors now; it SKIPS null targets with a warning. Nothing here is fabricated.

correctness_floors.note: Non-capacity invariants — enforced on every run regardless of status (they need no hardware baseline). A run that drops requests or fails closed spuriously fails the gate immediately.

capacity_targets.note: null until measured (MCPRE-110, HITL). Once a baseline run on the declared hardware records numbers, set these to the release floor/ceilings derived from that baseline and flip status to 'declared'.

scaling_targets.note: Per-core linear-scaling tolerance for the 1→N cores curve: achieved_N_core_throughput / (single_core_throughput * N) must be >= per_core_min_linear_factor. Requires a 1-core run and an N-core run on the same declared hardware (MCP_RE_LOADGEN_CORES=1 then =N). null until measured.
